summary: Iteration 11 verified end-to-end. Backend (15/15 pytest pass): (1) POST /api/sql/execute with no custom_schema runs SELECTs against built-in employees/departments/orders/logs (Grace Lee top engineer 110000, employees count=15). (2) POST /api/sql/execute with custom_schema executes CREATE TABLE+INSERT then runs SELECT on the new table; products GROUP BY returns Electronics(1)/Stationery(2). (3) Custom schema runs ALONGSIDE built-in tables (custom xyz + employees count=15 in same call). (4) Bad custom_schema returns {error:'Schema error: ...'} payload (200, not 500). (5) DROP DATABASE blocked with 400. (6) /api/sql/execute requires auth (401). (7) POST /api/upload/pdf accepts a real fpdf2-generated PDF and returns {text, pages>=1, filename}. (8) Non-PDF (text/plain) rejected with 400 'Only PDF files are allowed'. (9) /api/upload/pdf requires auth (401). (10) POST /api/interviews/start accepts pdf_context+restrict_to_pdf and persists them on the interview document. (11-14) POST /api/code/save upserts a draft, GET /api/code/draft/{problem_id} returns it (TEST_DRAFT_v2 verified), GET on missing returns {draft:null}, GET /api/submissions/solved-ids returns {solved_ids:[...]}. (15) /api/code/save requires auth. Frontend: SQL Lab (/sql) shows [data-testid=toggle-custom-schema] reading 'Add Custom Schema'; click flips to 'Custom Schema ON' with cyan background and reveals a second Monaco editor (CUSTOM SCHEMA (CREATE TABLE + INSERT)) with Load Example button; toggling OFF removes the schema editor (monaco count 2->1). Run footer text correctly says 'Custom schema + built-in tables' when ON. Interview setup (/interview) shows 'ATTACH REFERENCE MATERIAL (OPTIONAL)' card with [data-testid=pdf-upload-area]; uploading a real PDF via [data-testid=pdf-file-input] succeeds — filename and 'chars extracted' indicator render and [data-testid=restrict-to-pdf-toggle] checkbox appears.

minor.issue: On valid syntax-error in user's main query, response returns 200 with {error: ...}; on schema error also 200. Inconsistent with HTTPException(400) used for DROP DATABASE/EXEC etc. Consider 200 across the board for query errors or 400 for both — currently a mix.

ui_bugs.issue: 'chars extracted' shows '0.0k' for small PDFs because of (length/1000).toFixed(1). For a ~46-char PDF user sees '0.0k chars extracted' which looks like extraction failed. Display chars directly (e.g. '46 chars') when length < 1000.

action_items: (Optional) Standardize error contract for /api/sql/execute — pick 200+{error} OR 400 for both query-syntax errors and forbidden patterns; today schema/query errors return 200 while DROP DATABASE returns 400.
(Cosmetic) AIInterview.jsx line ~430: replace `${(pdfText.length / 1000).toFixed(1)}k chars extracted` with conditional that shows raw chars when <1000 to avoid '0.0k chars' for short PDFs.
Carry-over: server.py is now ~1230+ lines after PDF/draft/custom-schema additions; modularize into routers (sql, interviews, code, uploads).
Carry-over from iter8: streak calc in /api/profile/stats queries interview_messages without user_id filter; latent bug.
Carry-over: THREE.Clock deprecation warning still emitted by ThreeBackground/DSAVisualizer.
critical_code_review_comments: /api/upload/pdf: `len(reader.pages)` is referenced inside the return after the try/except — if the except path runs, `reader` may be undefined and a 400 is raised, fine. But on success path, large PDFs are read fully into memory and full text is concatenated then sliced to 50k chars — for a 10MB scientific PDF this can briefly hold tens of MB in process RAM per request. Consider streaming/aborting once 50k chars reached.

/api/sql/execute: opens a fresh in-memory SQLite per request and applies custom_schema each call — fine for now but no caching/per-user persistence; document that custom tables are ephemeral so users don't expect state across runs.

InterviewCreate.pdf_context has no length guard at API layer; build_system_prompt slices to 8000 chars, but the document is still persisted at full size in MongoDB. Add server-side cap (e.g., 50k) on pdf_context.

SQLPlayground.jsx: customSchema editor textarea is rendered above the query editor; the .monaco-editor textarea ordering is relied upon implicitly by tests — adding data-testid=schema-editor-textarea / query-editor-textarea on the Monaco wrappers would harden tests.

AIInterview.jsx handlePdfUpload: no client-side size check before POST — backend rejects >10MB but user sees a loader for the full upload first. Add a quick `file.size > 10*1024*1024` guard with toast.
/api/code/save accepts arbitrary problem_id (no existence check). Drafts can pile up for invalid IDs. Add 404 if dsa_problems lacks the id — or keep permissive but document it.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (toggle-custom-schema flips ON/OFF and reveals/hides schema editor; pdf-upload-area + pdf-file-input + restrict-to-pdf-toggle all functional with real PDF upload)

seed_data_creation: Generated /tmp/ui_test.pdf via fpdf2 for UI upload test. Created TEST_DRAFT_v2 code draft for prob_00001 (left in DB; small footprint).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 11 fully verified. Tests in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration11.py cover: SQL custom schema (positive, alongside built-in, bad schema, dangerous block, auth), PDF upload (valid via fpdf2, non-PDF rejected, auth), interview pdf_context+restrict_to_pdf persistence, code drafts (save/get/missing/auth), and solved-ids endpoint. Frontend route for SQL Playground is /sql (NOT /sql-playground - nav label is 'SQL Lab'). data-testids confirmed: toggle-custom-schema, pdf-upload-area, pdf-file-input, restrict-to-pdf-toggle, remove-pdf-btn. Note: /api/sql/execute returns 200 with {error:...} for bad SQL/schema (not 400). PDF char-count UI shows '0.0k chars extracted' for small PDFs (cosmetic only).
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs found. All requested features (custom SQL schema CREATE+INSERT+query, PDF upload with text extraction, interview PDF context+restriction, code drafts, solved-ids) work correctly end-to-end.
